label: author Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy@v1 (trust-proxy technique; Originator DJ Thomson)
script_source:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""15_encode_sovereign_attribution.py - author the overarching identity technique as Pathways.

Encodes Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy@v1 - the technique by which a
collaboration bundle acts as a TRUST PROXY for every author in its lineage: identity masking
AS attestation (sovereign proof + full attribution + forward rights + privacy). Authored by
the Originator (DJ Thomson). Composes the existing mechanic-level identity pathways and adds
the forward-rights / inherited-constraints / cryptographic-lineage dimensions. Also registers
the new template in the canon pathways-index.

Producing pathway: Collaboration.Meta.EmbedTechniqueProvenance@v1
Stdlib only. No network. No real names.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

SNAP = Path("collaboration/20260528-130500")
PW_DIR = SNAP / "collaboration-pathways" / "pathways"
INDEX = SNAP / "collaboration-pathways" / "pathways-index.yaml"
NEW_FILE = PW_DIR / "Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy.v1.yaml"

TEMPLATE = """# Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy@v1
pathway_template:
  identity:
    template_id: Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy@v1
    artifact_type: pathway-template
    triple: { domain: Collaboration, subdomain: Identity, action: SovereignAttributionProxy }
    version: 1.0.0
    authored_by: "Originator (DJ Thomson)"
    author_note: |
      First canonical Pathways encoding of the identity-masking + attestation-lockbox technique.
      Authored by the Originator (DJ Thomson). MikeOSS is, implicitly, the first collaborator whose
      invitation occasioned this encoding.

  description: |
    The overarching technique by which a collaboration bundle becomes a TRUST PROXY for every author
    in its lineage. It masks each real identity behind a stable placeholder, then seals a verifiable
    attestation binding that placeholder to a real DID and/or public identity anchor - so a cold
    reader can trust, BY INFERENCE, that real, unique people and entities stand behind the work,
    without any name being exposed. Each masked author retains:
      (1) SOVEREIGN PROOF of participation - self-asserted Personhood / Entity Credential + a salted
          public commitment sha256(real_name || salt) that a revealed name can later be checked against;
      (2) FULL ATTRIBUTION of all Pathways-encoded lineage and provenance underpinning the embedded /
          referenced pathways - on reveal, the run ledger, fork lineage, and Term edges resolve to them;
      (3) FORWARD RIGHTS - sovereign control over future reveal AND future actions of that identity,
          exercisable later via their own key (the 1-of-2 upgrade path), optionally within INHERITED
          CONSTRAINTS carried as explicit, cryptographically-lineaged terms;
      (4) PRIVACY - nothing reveals without a holder of the reveal key.
    This is identity masking AS attestation: privacy and attribution at once, with sovereign forward
    control and clear cryptographic lineage.

  composes:
    - Collaboration.Identity.IssuePersonhoodCredential@v1   # PHC / Entity Credential + salted commitment
    - Collaboration.Identity.SealNameLockbox@v1             # 1-of-2 reveal lockbox (AES-256-CBC / PBKDF2)
    - Collaboration.Identity.AssertRelationship@v1          # VRC: person <-> entity association
    - Collaboration.UX.RenderRevealViewer@v1                # the trust-proxy reveal UX (browser, client-side)
    - Collaboration.Identity.RevealLockbox@v1               # selective / progressive reveal

  inputs:
    - { name: subject, type: dict, description: "real name + DIDs / public identity anchors (held outside the seal, gitignored)" }
    - { name: placeholder, type: string, description: "stable de-identified id (e.g. person-a / entity-1)" }
    - { name: inherited_constraints, type: list, default: [], description: "terms that bind FUTURE actions of the revealed identity" }

  steps:
    - id: mask_identity
      agent_id: persist
      skill: deidentify_to_placeholder
      config: { map_source: "keys/subjects.private.json (gitignored, never sealed)" }
    - id: anchor_attestation
      agent_id: persist
      invokes: [Collaboration.Identity.IssuePersonhoodCredential]
      config:
        binds: "placeholder -> real DID / public identity anchor"
        commitment: "sha256(real_name || salt)   # public, name-free"
        scaffold: "OpenVTC-compatible PHC / Entity Credential (did:webvh deferred)"
    - id: seal_reveal_lockbox
      agent_id: persist
      invokes: [Collaboration.Identity.SealNameLockbox]
      config:
        reveal_policy: "1-of-2"
        keys: ["Originator (operative now)", "subject DID key (sovereign upgrade path)"]
    - id: assert_relationships
      agent_id: persist
      invokes: [Collaboration.Identity.AssertRelationship]
      config: { credential: "VRC (Verifiable Relationship Credential)" }
    - id: grant_forward_rights
      agent_id: persist
      skill: bind_forward_rights
      config:
        reveal_control: subject_sovereign        # the subject's key alone can reveal in future
        action_control: subject_sovereign        # future actions of the identity are the subject's to authorize
        inherited_constraints_apply: true         # within terms carried as cryptographic-lineage edges
        lineage: "edges from this attestation to every referenced pathway + PathwayRun it underpins"
    - id: render_proxy_viewer
      agent_id: composer
      invokes: [Collaboration.UX.RenderRevealViewer]

  forward_rights:
    sovereign_reveal: "Each author may reveal their own name / DID at any time via their key; the Originator's key is the interim 1-of-2 convenience, never a lock-out."
    sovereign_future_action: "Future actions taken under the revealed identity remain the author's to authorize, optionally bounded by inherited_constraints."
    inherited_constraints: "Constraints (if any) travel as explicit, signed terms with a cryptographic lineage back to this attestation; they bind forks, not the person's autonomy outside this context."
    cryptographic_lineage: "Reveal resolves the placeholder to a DID and to every Pathways-encoded provenance record (runs, forks, Terms) attributable to that author."

  scaffolding:
    prototype: "OpenVTC - https://github.com/OpenVTC/openvtc (DIDs, VCs, Personhood Credentials, Verifiable Relationship Credentials, did:webvh)."
    future_integration: "First Person Project / network - https://firstperson.network (first-person trust relationships; PHC / VRC interop)."
    note: "This bundle ships the credential SHAPES + lockboxes as OpenVTC-compatible scaffolding; a production deployment would issue did:webvh Persona DIDs and exchange VRCs over DIDComm."

  output:
    artifact: sovereign_attribution_proxy
    includes: [personhood_or_entity_credentials, name_lockboxes, relationship_credentials, reveal_viewer, forward_rights_grant]

  ethos:
    principle: "privacy-and-attribution-at-once; sovereign forward control; clear cryptographic lineage"
    pathways_home: "https://tesseractstakes.com/pathways"
"""

INDEX_ENTRY = """  - id: Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy
    file: pathways/Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy.v1.yaml
    new_in_canon: true
    authored_by: Originator (DJ Thomson)
    note: "Trust-proxy technique: identity masking AS attestation - sovereign proof, full attribution, forward rights, privacy."
    invokes:
      - Collaboration.Identity.IssuePersonhoodCredential
      - Collaboration.Identity.SealNameLockbox
      - Collaboration.Identity.AssertRelationship
      - Collaboration.UX.RenderRevealViewer
      - Collaboration.Identity.RevealLockbox

"""


def main() -> int:
    NEW_FILE.write_text(TEMPLATE, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"wrote {NEW_FILE}")

    idx = INDEX.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    if "Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy" not in idx:
        anchor = "\npatterns:\n"
        if anchor not in idx:
            raise SystemExit("could not find 'patterns:' anchor in pathways-index.yaml")
        idx = idx.replace(anchor, "\n" + INDEX_ENTRY + "patterns:\n", 1)
        INDEX.write_text(idx, encoding="utf-8")
        print(f"registered new pathway in {INDEX}")
    else:
        print("pathway already registered; index unchanged")

    produced = [
        str(NEW_FILE),
        str(INDEX),
        str(SNAP / "collaboration-pathways" / "scripts" / "15_encode_sovereign_attribution.py"),
    ]
    am = os.environ.get("ASSET_MANIFEST")
    if am:
        Path(am).write_text(json.dumps(produced), encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"sovereign-attribution technique encoded: {len(produced)} assets")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
stdout:
wrote collaboration/20260528-130500/collaboration-pathways/pathways/Collaboration.Identity.SovereignAttributionProxy.v1.yaml
registered new pathway in collaboration/20260528-130500/collaboration-pathways/pathways-index.yaml
sovereign-attribution technique encoded: 3 assets
